title: T-042 — PWA install + offline-shell Playwright E2E
prompt:
# T-042 — PWA install + offline-shell Playwright E2E

PRD: `00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §5 frontend (PWA Workbox), §13 (06-SCANNER-P1 PWA acceptance)
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`

## Goal
Playwright E2E proving the PWA scaffold from T-041 actually works end-to-end: manifest is served, service worker activates, and the app shell loads when network is offline.

## Implementation contract
(1) Create apps/web/e2e/pwa.spec.ts using Playwright with Chromium service-worker support. (2) Test 1 navigates to /, asserts the manifest link tag and fetches /manifest.webmanifest, checks JSON contains name='Monopilot'. (3) Test 2 waits for navigator.serviceWorker.ready, then page.context().setOffline(true), then page.reload() — asserts the page still renders the layout (cached app shell). (4) Test 3 hits /api/health twice online (warm cache), then setOffline(true) + a third fetch — asserts the response comes from the SW cache (status 200, custom header X-Served-By='sw'). (5) Add 'pnpm test:e2e:pwa' script.

## Files (scope)
- apps/web/e2e/pwa.spec.ts [create]
- apps/web/app/api/health/route.ts [create]
- apps/web/package.json [modify]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the production build is running under Playwright, when /manifest.webmanifest is fetched, then it returns 200 with content-type containing 'manifest+json' and JSON name='Monopilot'
2. Given the SW has activated, when the test sets offline and reloads /, then the layout still renders (cached app shell) within 3 seconds
3. Given /api/health was hit twice online, when offline + third fetch runs, then the SW returns the cached response with header X-Served-By='sw' and status 200
4. Given 'pnpm --filter web test:e2e:pwa' runs in CI, when the spec exits, then it exits 0 with all 3 tests green

## Test strategy
- RED: write apps/web/e2e/pwa.spec.ts (the spec file) before adjusting the SW to add the X-Served-By header — it must fail first, then pass after the SW change in T-041 sw.ts is extended
- Run 'pnpm --filter web test:e2e:pwa'

## Risk red lines
- Do not run the PWA E2E in dev mode — only against production build (matches PRD §5 'PWA only in production')
- Do not stub navigator.serviceWorker — use Playwright's real Chromium SW support
- Do not extend the test to cover IndexedDB sync queue — that is T-044 scope

## Dependencies (must be complete first)
- T-041

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED test before implementation), verification-before-completion, and requesting-code-review at GREEN.

pipeline_inputs.description: Playwright E2E proving the T-041 PWA scaffold serves manifest, activates SW, and serves app shell + /api/health from cache when offline.
